papers.title_en: Federated learning with heavy-tailed gradient noise and communication noise: a variance-reduction based algorithm
papers.evidence: 针对通信噪声的联邦学习方差缩减算法
papers.tldr: 联邦学习在无线网络和物联网部署中常受重尾梯度噪声和通信噪声干扰，影响模型收敛。本文提出VRA-FedSGD算法，采用动量方差缩减与非线性映射抑制重尾梯度噪声，并通过方差缩减聚合机制消除通信噪声。理论分析表明，非凸目标下收敛率为\mathcal{O}(K^{-(p-1)/(2p-1)})，强凸目标下几乎必然收敛率为\tilde{\mathcal{O}}(K^{-(1-1/(p-\epsilon))})。在逻辑回归问题上的实验验证了算法有效性，为实际联邦学习系统提供了鲁棒解决方案。
papers.abstract_en: Federated learning (FL) is an emerging distributed machine learning paradigm that enables local devices to jointly train a global model while keeping data decentralized and private. We propose a variance-reduction based algorithm, VRA-FedSGD, for FL in the presence of heavy-tailed gradient noise and communication noise, where these noises are prevalent in large-scale machine learning over wireless networks and Internet of Things deployments. VRA-FedSGD employs a momentum variance reduction technique together with a nonlinear mapping to mitigate heavy-tailed gradient noise, and uses a variance-reduced aggregation mechanism to suppress heavy-tailed communication noise. In the mean sense, VRA-FedSGD achieves a convergence rate of {\small$\mathcal{O}\left(K^{-(p-1)/(2p-1)}\right)$} for nonconvex objective functions, where $p$ is the tail index of heavy-tailed noise. In the almost sure sense, VRA-FedSGD achieves a convergence rate of $\tilde{\mathcal{O}}\left(K^{-(1-1/(p-ε))}\right)$ for strongly convex objective functions, where $ε$ is an arbitrarily small constant. Simulated experiments on a logistic regression problem with real-world data verify the effectiveness of VRA-FedSGD.

papers.title_en: Semi-asynchronous Federated Learning in Flower: Framework Extension and Performance Assessment
papers.authors: Víctor Hidalgo-Izquierdo, Carmen Carrión, Blanca Caminero
papers.evidence: 半异步联邦学习处理客户端异构和掉队者
papers.tldr: 联邦学习面临客户端异构性和掉队者问题，传统同步机制效率低下。本文在Flower框架中扩展了半异步联邦学习策略FedSaSync，允许客户端部分同步，减少空闲等待。实验表明，该方法在收敛稳定性与系统效率之间取得平衡，显著提升了异构环境下的训练鲁棒性和吞吐量。该工作为实际部署提供了实用的框架扩展。
papers.abstract_en: This paper presents an extension of the Flower federated learning framework to support Semi-Asynchronous Federated Learning. The proposed approach adapts the traditional synchronous paradigm to better handle client heterogeneity and straggler effects. By introducing a semi-asynchronous training strategy, the system allows partial synchronization among clients while maintaining training efficiency and scalability. We implement and evaluate the proposed modification within Flower, instantiated as the FedSaSync strategy, demonstrating improved robustness and reduced idle time compared to fully synchronous baselines in heterogeneous environments. The results show that SAFL can balance convergence stability and system efficiency in heterogeneous environments typical of edge and distributed learning scenarios.
